title: Putin faces a series of setbacks, fueling discontent in Russia and raising doubts about his strategy
subhead: While the Russian president claims that victory in Ukraine is within reach, Kyiv is stepping up drone strikes deep inside Russian territory. These military setbacks compound the country's mounting economic difficulties.

title: Sweden's right-wing and far-right coalition backs down on deportations of young adults
Trump says Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt fighting
Leading AI firm Anthropic files confidentially for IPO
subhead: The company, which develops Claude, is positioning itself to tap public markets as leading artificial intelligence companies seek vast new funding to expand computing capacity.

title: French government restricts Israel's official participation in the Eurosatory defense trade show
subhead: Only companies exhibiting missile defense or anti-aircraft systems are allowed. The Israeli defense ministry is protesting what it calls a 'disgraceful' decision, which was already enforced at the Paris Air Show in June 2025.

title: Public debate begins on the 'Higgs factory,' Europe's future particle accelerator
subhead: The future circular collider at CERN, intended to probe the properties of the Higgs boson in 20 years' time, excites physicists. Yet its construction, beneath the surface of France and Switzerland, is certain to impact the environment and local residents. For that reason, a public debate on this colossal project begins on June 2 and will continue for several months.

title: The Fontainebleau Forest's ever-growing popularity threatens this jewel of biodiversity near Paris
subhead: Managers of the forest complex are wondering how to deal with the increasing influx of visitors: Will development contain overtourism or encourage it?

The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhead: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

title: Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
subhead: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

title: French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
subhead: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.
